visual_notes: Characteristic waxy luster on polished surfaces. Translucent with a faint glow in thin sections. Smooth, ghost-like quality distinguishes it from coarse quartz.
vibe_notes: Chalcedony reads as quiet, cool, and internally lit. The waxy surface has a specific tactile quality — smooth and almost skin-like. The blue form evokes fog, still water, and negative space.
folklore_summary: Carnelian (a chalcedony variety) was one of the most important gem materials of the ancient world, used in amulets, seals, and jewelry across Egypt, Mesopotamia, and Rome.
